name: Bailey, Thomas Craig
motto: The smile that was childlike and bland.
description: "Tom" arrived in town a few weeks late and was discovered wandering aimlessly about "T" hall. He soon got acquainted, however, and then tried to pet the buzz-planer. After this "T Craig" got all he wanted of engineering, so he changed to Arts and Science. But he is still mechanically inclined, and takes great pride in the automobile which he has completely reconstructed. After many experiments he found that a connecting rod put in wrong end to would not work well, so the machine was put away for the winter. Tom expects to enter his car in the Vanderbilt race this year.
text:
The smile that was childlike and bland.
"Tom" arrived in town a few weeks late and was discovered wandering aimlessly about "T" hall. He soon got acquainted, however, and then tried to pet the buzz-planer. After this "T Craig" got all he wanted of engineering, so he changed to Arts and Science. But he is still mechanically inclined, and takes great pride in the automobile which he has completely reconstructed. After many experiments he found that a connecting rod put in wrong end to would not work well, so the machine was put away for the winter. Tom expects to enter his car in the Vanderbilt race this year.

name: Berry, George Wesley
motto: Silken, chaste and hushed.
description: Because of his modesty, George has only recently become well acquainted with all of us. As some of us know he is decidedly against co-education and as assistant editor of the Monthly, we expect him to write an article on this subject soon. Nevertheless, George has a great mind, and we predict an illustrious future, either as governor or as editor of a farmer's Gazette.
text:
Silken, chaste and hushed.
Because of his modesty, George has only recently become well acquainted with all of us. As some of us know he is decidedly against co-education and as assistant editor of the Monthly, we expect him to write an article on this subject soon. Nevertheless, George has a great mind, and we predict an illustrious future, either as governor or as editor of a farmer's Gazette.

name: Bradford, Maurice Palmer
motto: Silence is golden.
description: "Brad" never did say much, he slid in sidewise and generally kept out of the sun for fear he would cast a shadow. There are several things for which we can never forgive him, but we will only mention the least of these. During his Freshman year he was manager of the Pettee-Bradford Water Works, and then the land did flow with oil and water. This kind of fire water did not agree with our health, but "Brad" allowed it was oil right. However, he intends to perfect a method by which gasoline can be dissolved in water without detection. Here's hoping.
text:
Silence is golden.
"Brad" never did say much, he slid in sidewise and generally kept out of the sun for fear he would cast a shadow. There are several things for which we can never forgive him, but we will only mention the least of these. During his Freshman year he was manager of the Pettee-Bradford Water Works, and then the land did flow with oil and water. This kind of fire water did not agree with our health, but "Brad" allowed it was oil right. However, he intends to perfect a method by which gasoline can be dissolved in water without detection. Here's hoping.

name: Buckminster, Paul Demeritt
motto: The world agrees that he writes well.
description: This heterogeneous solid phase of variable composition is commonly known to the universe at large as P. D. Buckminster, but to us who know his every thought and feeling "the most worthy Duke" seems to apply nicely. He is a Chemical man, but since he wields the blue pencil in this book we shall have to pass him to First. The "Duke" played an errorless game until "Artie" knocked down a stiff liner in the form of "Least Squares," which "Buck" juggled considerably. However, he finally caught "Artie" off third and prevented him from scoring.
text:
The world agrees that he writes well.
This heterogeneous solid phase of variable composition is commonly known to the universe at large as P. D. Buckminster, but to us who know his every thought and feeling "the most worthy Duke" seems to apply nicely. He is a Chemical man, but since he wields the blue pencil in this book we shall have to pass him to First. The "Duke" played an errorless game until "Artie" knocked down a stiff liner in the form of "Least Squares," which "Buck" juggled considerably. However, he finally caught "Artie" off third and prevented him from scoring.

name: Bunker, Lewis La Forrest Harold
motto: She is mine own And I as rich — as twenty seas.
description: "Bunk" is afflicted with a long name, and a love for "E. E.," but he is not to blame, he can't help it. He is putting in some solid licks, and is one of our star students. Rumor has it that he has an aim in view, and will set up in business with a side partner soon after graduation. Here's to his happiness.
text:
She is mine own And I as rich — as twenty seas.
"Bunk" is afflicted with a long name, and a love for "E. E.," but he is not to blame, he can't help it. He is putting in some solid licks, and is one of our star students. Rumor has it that he has an aim in view, and will set up in business with a side partner soon after graduation. Here's to his happiness.

name: Catlin, Harwood Baldwin
motto: The soul of art best loved when love is by.
description: "Cat" is taking his college work in courses. He had his first year course here, then his second at Harvard, and now he is back here for his third. It is expected that he will go to Vassar for his final one and his degree. We made a mistake; he takes his regular work in Dover, and his supplementary courses here. Harwood's ties are of every size, and also of every hue. The bow, puff and teck, they all fit his neck. Too bad that he cannot wear two.
text:
The soul of art best loved when love is by.
"Cat" is taking his college work in courses. He had his first year course here, then his second at Harvard, and now he is back here for his third. It is expected that he will go to Vassar for his final one and his degree. We made a mistake; he takes his regular work in Dover, and his supplementary courses here. Harwood's ties are of every size, and also of every hue. The bow, puff and teck, they all fit his neck. Too bad that he cannot wear two.

name: Cole, Florence Viola
motto: The joy of my life is a library.
description: The ordinary courses do not just appeal to Florence's temperament. A special course, "Phil '13," seems to satisfy her taste better than any other; a fact to which the many hours spent in library reference work can testify. But once in a while under the inspiration of the Muses she gives us something worth while, like the "Line Up." Consequently, we are glad to have her with us.
text:
The joy of my life is a library.
The ordinary courses do not just appeal to Florence's temperament. A special course, "Phil '13," seems to satisfy her taste better than any other; a fact to which the many hours spent in library reference work can testify. But once in a while under the inspiration of the Muses she gives us something worth while, like the "Line Up." Consequently, we are glad to have her with us.

name: Crosby, Percy Raymond
motto: None but himself can be his parallel.
description: Percy said, after thinking it over for two and a half years, that "he couldn't see anything that he wanted in engineering." There is an old saying that large bodies move slowly. Thought is not slow, but it takes time for even rapid thought to percolate a large body. However, Percy has certainly shown us that he made a wise move. We admire his talent and appreciate the work which he has done for this volume.
text:
None but himself can be his parallel.
Percy said, after thinking it over for two and a half years, that "he couldn't see anything that he wanted in engineering." There is an old saying that large bodies move slowly. Thought is not slow, but it takes time for even rapid thought to percolate a large body. However, Percy has certainly shown us that he made a wise move. We admire his talent and appreciate the work which he has done for this volume.

name: Davis, Arthur Grant
motto: Independence now and independence forever.
description: "Dave" and the governor come from the same town, and rumor has it that when the latter was elected "Dave" stepped up, slapped him on the back, and said, "Fine work, old boy, we'll pull well together." However, "Dave's" chief attention here is turned toward the military department, he swears by the lieutenant, and it was by his express wish that he was promoted this year from first sergeant to musician. Some day he is bound to be captain of the New Hampshire coast artillery with headquarters on Lake Winnipesaukee.
text:
Independence now and independence forever.
"Dave" and the governor come from the same town, and rumor has it that when the latter was elected "Dave" stepped up, slapped him on the back, and said, "Fine work, old boy, we'll pull well together." However, "Dave's" chief attention here is turned toward the military department, he swears by the lieutenant, and it was by his express wish that he was promoted this year from first sergeant to musician. Some day he is bound to be captain of the New Hampshire coast artillery with headquarters on Lake Winnipesaukee.

name: Davison, Frank Selden
motto: O, it is excellent To have a giant's strength.
description: Since the morning when we as freshmen pulled down the posters, "Ikey" has been in the game. During freshman and sophomore years a word was enough for a class scrap for our chesty boy. "Davie" is reported to have a particular fondness for barbed wire fences and "Artie's" physics. But as for the latter, he says he does not see how he can apply the angle theta to football, nor the density of the atmosphere to plowing a field, therefore, he begs to be excused. He is an artist, too, and it is said that he prefers "studies in Browns."
text:
O, it is excellent To have a giant's strength.
Since the morning when we as freshmen pulled down the posters, "Ikey" has been in the game. During freshman and sophomore years a word was enough for a class scrap for our chesty boy. "Davie" is reported to have a particular fondness for barbed wire fences and "Artie's" physics. But as for the latter, he says he does not see how he can apply the angle theta to football, nor the density of the atmosphere to plowing a field, therefore, he begs to be excused. He is an artist, too, and it is said that he prefers "studies in Browns."

name: Demeritt, Stephen
motto: What is the end of study? Let me know.
description: "Steve" says that so long as a man can get by without study, just so long he ("Steve") is going to do it. He did;— but now he is laboring a little at his "P. Lab." and such. As for his favorite occupation, that seems to be selling class canes; he says it's a fine business, but the greatest drawback is that there is no money in it. In later life "Steve" expects to perfect a system for doing the farm work at Broad Acres without labor. We wish him success.
text:
What is the end of study? Let me know.
"Steve" says that so long as a man can get by without study, just so long he ("Steve") is going to do it. He did;— but now he is laboring a little at his "P. Lab." and such. As for his favorite occupation, that seems to be selling class canes; he says it's a fine business, but the greatest drawback is that there is no money in it. In later life "Steve" expects to perfect a system for doing the farm work at Broad Acres without labor. We wish him success.

name: Donnelly, Edith Gertrude
motto: The blushing beauty of a modest maid.
description: "Kiddo" came to college from her native hamlet, Dover, and was a quiet little thing in a blue "Peter Thompson." When the year was over she was given the quill and scroll of the class, for it was then that we began to realize her abilities. Her work this year as art editor of the GRANITE has certainly been most praiseworthy. Edith is fond of dancing, and we know that she always does her best to have the informals come as often as possible. "Kiddo" says that she is going to teach after she gets her diploma. Here's to her success!
text:
The blushing beauty of a modest maid.
"Kiddo" came to college from her native hamlet, Dover, and was a quiet little thing in a blue "Peter Thompson." When the year was over she was given the quill and scroll of the class, for it was then that we began to realize her abilities. Her work this year as art editor of the GRANITE has certainly been most praiseworthy. Edith is fond of dancing, and we know that she always does her best to have the informals come as often as possible. "Kiddo" says that she is going to teach after she gets her diploma. Here's to her success!

name: Drew, Lyle Stevens
motto: Give thy thoughts no tongue.
description: They do say that the year Lyle spent at Norwich nearly cost him his gift of speech. Anyhow, he quietly slipped in here, our Sophomore year, gently whispered that he was one of us, and then relapsed once more into quietude. From this state he rarely arouses himself except to visit Smith Hall. Cheer up, Lyle, the race does not go to the man with the most gas, but to the one who can hang on longest.
text:
Give thy thoughts no tongue.
They do say that the year Lyle spent at Norwich nearly cost him his gift of speech. Anyhow, he quietly slipped in here, our Sophomore year, gently whispered that he was one of us, and then relapsed once more into quietude. From this state he rarely arouses himself except to visit Smith Hall. Cheer up, Lyle, the race does not go to the man with the most gas, but to the one who can hang on longest.

name: Fisher, Frank Gordon
motto: I'll fight till from my bones the flesh be hacked.
description: "Fish" was the man who started things moving when our verdant class first struck town, but he didn't belong to us then, oh no! He promptly picked out "Ikey" and waded in for a fight. "He was a bold, bad man," and had us going for a while. He's tame now though, and will eat from the hand. A year out in the cold, cold world, with rising hour at five A. M., does wonders for one.
text:
I'll fight till from my bones the flesh be hacked.
"Fish" was the man who started things moving when our verdant class first struck town, but he didn't belong to us then, oh no! He promptly picked out "Ikey" and waded in for a fight. "He was a bold, bad man," and had us going for a while. He's tame now though, and will eat from the hand. A year out in the cold, cold world, with rising hour at five A. M., does wonders for one.

name: Foster, Leland Stanley
motto: Whence is thy learning? Hath thy toil O'er books consumed the midnight oil?
description: Midst the green hills of central New Hampshire "Slats" grew up into "manhood's estate," and then he came to us. We knew he was a great man at first glance: his gait told us so. To look at him as he went down the street one would say that the town was under his thumb. He fooled the faculty for a while, but "Sub. Nesbit" finally nailed him, and now "Slats" is entered for the relay race.
text:
Whence is thy learning? Hath thy toil O'er books consumed the midnight oil?
Midst the green hills of central New Hampshire "Slats" grew up into "manhood's estate," and then he came to us. We knew he was a great man at first glance: his gait told us so. To look at him as he went down the street one would say that the town was under his thumb. He fooled the faculty for a while, but "Sub. Nesbit" finally nailed him, and now "Slats" is entered for the relay race.

name: Gowen, Philip Lewis
motto: Love can make what shape he pleases.
description: "Phil" certainly has got an awful "bean" on him; he knows "E.E." from "M.E." and all such things. Really, no one would expect to find such a bunch of knowledge so far above ground over those long legs and that soldierly body, that bends and waves as the willow. We predict that he is a second Abraham Lincoln without the fire-light and the charcoal on the snow shovel. Watch out for a chemical explosion some day. "Herr Myer" will be in its midst with an everpresent smile.
text:
Love can make what shape he pleases.
"Phil" certainly has got an awful "bean" on him; he knows "E.E." from "M.E." and all such things. Really, no one would expect to find such a bunch of knowledge so far above ground over those long legs and that soldierly body, that bends and waves as the willow. We predict that he is a second Abraham Lincoln without the fire-light and the charcoal on the snow shovel. Watch out for a chemical explosion some day. "Herr Myer" will be in its midst with an everpresent smile.

name: Hayes, Bernice Marion
motto: Character is worth a thousand laurels.
description: Bernice is a native of old Durham town, and since childhood has ever been so saturated with college activities that never has she been much enthused over her own college life. Therefore, her studies have ever been uppermost in her thoughts, and we shall always remember Bernice because of her brilliant head.
text:
Character is worth a thousand laurels.
Bernice is a native of old Durham town, and since childhood has ever been so saturated with college activities that never has she been much enthused over her own college life. Therefore, her studies have ever been uppermost in her thoughts, and we shall always remember Bernice because of her brilliant head.

name: Holden, Hiram Chester
motto: 'Tis well to be off with the old love Before you are on with the new.
description: One day in September, 1908, there arrived in Durham an unsophisticated freshman, Hiram Pie Eben Holden by name. He became a chemist, and thus explained satisfactorily his "affinity" for the fair sex. "Eb" is never happy unless he is within the charm of the "Dorm," and although he is always in some trouble or other, he never wants to get off "Scott free." It is said by those who are on the inside that a prominent sorority was named in his honor. Still he lives under the strain.
text:
'Tis well to be off with the old love Before you are on with the new.
One day in September, 1908, there arrived in Durham an unsophisticated freshman, Hiram Pie Eben Holden by name. He became a chemist, and thus explained satisfactorily his "affinity" for the fair sex. "Eb" is never happy unless he is within the charm of the "Dorm," and although he is always in some trouble or other, he never wants to get off "Scott free." It is said by those who are on the inside that a prominent sorority was named in his honor. Still he lives under the strain.

name: Jennings, Earle Brigham
motto:
Night after night
He sat and bleared his eyes with books.
description: "Jenny" is one of those men who "thought he would, then thought he wouldn't." He came with 1911, but "flew the coop" and stayed away a year. Now he is here with us. "Jenny" is a plugger, and sticks by "Cardie's" mechanics most of the time. We don't know anything bad about him, aside from that, and the fact that the "Lieut." and his department are favorites of his. We'll let him go at that.
text:
Night after night
He sat and bleared his eyes with books.
"Jenny" is one of those men who "thought he would, then thought he wouldn't." He came with 1911, but "flew the coop" and stayed away a year. Now he is here with us. "Jenny" is a plugger, and sticks by "Cardie's" mechanics most of the time. We don't know anything bad about him, aside from that, and the fact that the "Lieut." and his department are favorites of his. We'll let him go at that.

name: Leighton, Arthur John
motto: Curfew shall not ring to-night.
description: "Leight" gets us up for breakfast in the morning, up to college at 8 A. M., again at 1.30 P. M., and then lets us know that it is 6 o'clock and supper time. They say he has a strong pull - with the bell rope. Aside from that one failing we know nothing bad about him, so we can't say any more.
text:
Curfew shall not ring to-night.
"Leight" gets us up for breakfast in the morning, up to college at 8 A. M., again at 1.30 P. M., and then lets us know that it is 6 o'clock and supper time. They say he has a strong pull - with the bell rope. Aside from that one failing we know nothing bad about him, so we can't say any more.

name: Lovell, Roscoe Ernest
motto: He that hath a beard is more than a youth; And he that hath none is less than a man.
description: "Pilkson" "can't live without butter you know," and to that fact we place his still childlike countenance, but he says he does not care. "Pilk's" specialties are fiddling and contorting. Really, if you could see him in the midst of his serpentine twistings you would have no longer any wonder at the number of wailing notes he can draw from his fiddle. But after all, "Pilk" is a good boy, and we want to see him get by.
text:
He that hath a beard is more than a youth; And he that hath none is less than a man.
"Pilkson" "can't live without butter you know," and to that fact we place his still childlike countenance, but he says he does not care. "Pilk's" specialties are fiddling and contorting. Really, if you could see him in the midst of his serpentine twistings you would have no longer any wonder at the number of wailing notes he can draw from his fiddle. But after all, "Pilk" is a good boy, and we want to see him get by.

name: Lowd, Clarence Mortimer
motto: I should think your tongue had broken its chain.
description: "Whozis" is a big man from down the line, and has the choicest collection of expressive words of any one of us, only he has trouble in ejaculating them, consequently his "Who's-this." "Clancy" is pretty long on athletics and almost made the Varsity baseball team last year, but he more than did that in football and track. So you see it's up to us to lift our lids to "Cap'n Lowd" of both those teams. Just at present he's figuring hard on the question: "Why is least squares," or "The best way of getting by the course."
text:
I should think your tongue had broken its chain.
"Whozis" is a big man from down the line, and has the choicest collection of expressive words of any one of us, only he has trouble in ejaculating them, consequently his "Who's-this." "Clancy" is pretty long on athletics and almost made the Varsity baseball team last year, but he more than did that in football and track. So you see it's up to us to lift our lids to "Cap'n Lowd" of both those teams. Just at present he's figuring hard on the question: "Why is least squares," or "The best way of getting by the course."

name: Manter, Jerauld Armington
motto: Come, give us a taste of your quality.
description: The chief fault that "Jed" finds with the faculty is that they will not let him take the entire curriculum in one semester. It is too bad we know, but such a precedent might be taken as good for the rest of us, and then — well, we would hate to see a college with one student. For all his studious proclivities "Jed" is a good fellow, and rumbles a bass from the vicinity of his toes. Some day he expects to be principal and musical director of the Massabesic high school. It's well to aim high, "Jed."
text:
Come, give us a taste of your quality.
The chief fault that "Jed" finds with the faculty is that they will not let him take the entire curriculum in one semester. It is too bad we know, but such a precedent might be taken as good for the rest of us, and then — well, we would hate to see a college with one student. For all his studious proclivities "Jed" is a good fellow, and rumbles a bass from the vicinity of his toes. Some day he expects to be principal and musical director of the Massabesic high school. It's well to aim high, "Jed."

name: McLucas, Charles Abraham
motto: This is no place for a minister's son.
description: After two long years of deliberation "Mac" decided that "1912" was the best class in college. We quite agree with "Mac" and assure him that "1912" was made even better by his changing. The habit has become fixed on "Rusty" and he is deliberating what his chances are of passing off his "K" in Physics 3. "Mac" is studious and passed off twenty-nine hours in one semester. The question arises, how much richer were the members of the faculty?
text:
This is no place for a minister's son.
After two long years of deliberation "Mac" decided that "1912" was the best class in college. We quite agree with "Mac" and assure him that "1912" was made even better by his changing. The habit has become fixed on "Rusty" and he is deliberating what his chances are of passing off his "K" in Physics 3. "Mac" is studious and passed off twenty-nine hours in one semester. The question arises, how much richer were the members of the faculty?

name: McPheters, George Allen
motto: Whence is thy learning?
description: Our George hails from Portsmouth where he left the delicate odor of spring flowers as he came away to Durham. After two years he found that the attractions of engineering were nothing to him, so now he is hitting the high spots in "Billy's" courses, and bumping the hummocks in "Scotty's" courses. "Mac" is a "fine boy" and we wish him well. In the future we expect to see him doing female impersonating at the Old Howard, or a "sketch" with Doc. Shapleigh as side partner.
text:
Whence is thy learning?
Our George hails from Portsmouth where he left the delicate odor of spring flowers as he came away to Durham. After two years he found that the attractions of engineering were nothing to him, so now he is hitting the high spots in "Billy's" courses, and bumping the hummocks in "Scotty's" courses. "Mac" is a "fine boy" and we wish him well. In the future we expect to see him doing female impersonating at the Old Howard, or a "sketch" with Doc. Shapleigh as side partner.

name: Morgan, Ralph Clifford
motto: Make false hair, and thatch your poor, thin roof with burthens of the dead.
description: Old "Morg" pulled the wrong switch and finally wound up in Durham one sunny morning in September. "Morg" is a scientific man from the word go, and is now working ardently on the subject of Physics to find a method for producing hair on an ivory dome. Whether "Morg" is going or coming has been a subject of much discussion. However, a theory has been advanced that he has "A little movement all his own."
text:
Make false hair, and thatch your poor, thin roof with burthens of the dead.
Old "Morg" pulled the wrong switch and finally wound up in Durham one sunny morning in September. "Morg" is a scientific man from the word go, and is now working ardently on the subject of Physics to find a method for producing hair on an ivory dome. Whether "Morg" is going or coming has been a subject of much discussion. However, a theory has been advanced that he has "A little movement all his own."

name: Neal, Cecil Maurice
motto: Much ado about nothing.
description: "Doctor Angel-face" Neal is a very busy man, but it's hard to find out what he is busy doing, except for one thing. Eben says he is a finished "fusser," but we have no other knowledge of it. "Doc" gets "K" in most everything but Dormitory 1, 2, 3, and 4; so far in these courses he has received A+. For two years "Doc" labored with E.E., but now thinks that he will be a real "Doctor," if the weather holds good and it doesn't look like rain.
text:
Much ado about nothing.
"Doctor Angel-face" Neal is a very busy man, but it's hard to find out what he is busy doing, except for one thing. Eben says he is a finished "fusser," but we have no other knowledge of it. "Doc" gets "K" in most everything but Dormitory 1, 2, 3, and 4; so far in these courses he has received A+. For two years "Doc" labored with E.E., but now thinks that he will be a real "Doctor," if the weather holds good and it doesn't look like rain.

name: O'Malley, Michael Joseph
motto: Demure little thing — and so tame.
description: Mike owns up to coming from "Somersworth," but prefers to have it called "East Rochester;" it's "more classy you know." Mike "never done nothing to nobody" except to smile at them. He generally "allows as how" it's a fine day, and that "Scotty's" courses are "great." Here's how to "Mike!"
text:
Demure little thing — and so tame.
Mike owns up to coming from "Somersworth," but prefers to have it called "East Rochester;" it's "more classy you know." Mike "never done nothing to nobody" except to smile at them. He generally "allows as how" it's a fine day, and that "Scotty's" courses are "great." Here's how to "Mike!"

name: Perkins, Irving Clement
motto: The thunder, That deep and dreadful organ-pipe.
description: "Perk" let out one of his growls the first time he saw a Sophomore in Durham, and remained ready to eat 'em up until the end of the year. Then he changed around and was ready to do the trick to any Freshman who looked at him. But now he has lost the thunder of his voice and the "fair ones" have him safely ensnared. "Perk" expects to drop his trunk at the "Dorm" next year, and make that his headquarters.
text:
The thunder, That deep and dreadful organ-pipe.
"Perk" let out one of his growls the first time he saw a Sophomore in Durham, and remained ready to eat 'em up until the end of the year. Then he changed around and was ready to do the trick to any Freshman who looked at him. But now he has lost the thunder of his voice and the "fair ones" have him safely ensnared. "Perk" expects to drop his trunk at the "Dorm" next year, and make that his headquarters.

name: Robinson, John Everett
motto: "But the jingling of the guinea helps."
description: "Robbie" dropped in on us quietly, gradually began churning around in the puddle, making a spatter, and then we turned around and said, "Ah, there's the Bo." From that time John began to have charge of the coffers, and he has kept it up ever since. His other accomplishments are chemistry, and "slinging hash" at the "Hen House." This is the job that "Robbie" likes best, and he knows down to a fraction the number of molecules that it takes to satisfy a co-ed's appetite.
text:
"But the jingling of the guinea helps."
"Robbie" dropped in on us quietly, gradually began churning around in the puddle, making a spatter, and then we turned around and said, "Ah, there's the Bo." From that time John began to have charge of the coffers, and he has kept it up ever since. His other accomplishments are chemistry, and "slinging hash" at the "Hen House." This is the job that "Robbie" likes best, and he knows down to a fraction the number of molecules that it takes to satisfy a co-ed's appetite.

name: Rogers, William Edward
motto: "And still be doing, never done."
description: "Bill" is always busy and goes around with his chest out and arms swinging wildly. They say he got that habit in Freshman year, when, after doing several drawing plates for "Put," who was having them published, he got a "C" for the semester's mark in "Mechanical." "Bill" was so proud of that "C" that he stuck his chest out so far that he has never gotten it back to normal since.
text:
"And still be doing, never done."
"Bill" is always busy and goes around with his chest out and arms swinging wildly. They say he got that habit in Freshman year, when, after doing several drawing plates for "Put," who was having them published, he got a "C" for the semester's mark in "Mechanical." "Bill" was so proud of that "C" that he stuck his chest out so far that he has never gotten it back to normal since.

name: Sawyer, Arthur Herbert
motto: "In maiden meditation, fancy free."
description: "Art" is a quiet lad, now at least, but two years ago he made things rustle in a rough house. We can't seem to understand what has sobered him so much, but have decided to place it on the cares of home life. Girls are mean things to manage. "Gorham" is big enough for it, and if size counts he will be boss.
text:
"In maiden meditation, fancy free."
"Art" is a quiet lad, now at least, but two years ago he made things rustle in a rough house. We can't seem to understand what has sobered him so much, but have decided to place it on the cares of home life. Girls are mean things to manage. "Gorham" is big enough for it, and if size counts he will be boss.

name: Shapleigh, Edward Eugene
motto: Inspiring bold John Barleycorn, What dangers thou canst make us scorn.
description: "Doc" came among us quietly, saying no word without meditation, but later on it was discovered that he could "cuss" with no hesitation. He hails from "Kittery and Portsmouth," as the society columns of the newspapers say, and is quite a society man in his simple way.
text:
Inspiring bold John Barleycorn, What dangers thou canst make us scorn.
"Doc" came among us quietly, saying no word without meditation, but later on it was discovered that he could "cuss" with no hesitation. He hails from "Kittery and Portsmouth," as the society columns of the newspapers say, and is quite a society man in his simple way.

name: Skinner, Russell Emerson
motto: Beard was never the true standard of brains.
description: They say that "Billy" began shaving at the precocious age of twelve and then forgot the art for several years. At present he is just picking it up again, and does "scrape 'em off" once a week or so. "Rus" has agriculture and automobiles for his chief entertainments, but he says that working, just to keep your mind occupied, is like keeping boarders for company.
text:
Beard was never the true standard of brains.
They say that "Billy" began shaving at the precocious age of twelve and then forgot the art for several years. At present he is just picking it up again, and does "scrape 'em off" once a week or so. "Rus" has agriculture and automobiles for his chief entertainments, but he says that working, just to keep your mind occupied, is like keeping boarders for company.

name: Towle, George Wesley
motto: What a fine man Hath your tailor made you.
description: "Count" came over from Newmarket on the 8.12 as usual. The first thing he did was to register for E. E., and he has been specializing Math. I. ever since. "Count" has lately taken up Y. M. C. A. work, and it is rumored that he is doing a great good to the country in general. This is a fine line of work for the "millionaire baby."
text:
What a fine man Hath your tailor made you.
"Count" came over from Newmarket on the 8.12 as usual. The first thing he did was to register for E. E., and he has been specializing Math. I. ever since. "Count" has lately taken up Y. M. C. A. work, and it is rumored that he is doing a great good to the country in general. This is a fine line of work for the "millionaire baby."

name: Tucker, Herbert Ray
motto: An Angel! or if not, an earthly paragon.
clubs: Editor-in-chief Y. M. C. A. Handbook (2)
description: The inevitable whale heaved another sigh, wiggled its tail to and fro once or twice and behold! There on the salty seaweed stood Herb Tucker, perfection personified. This is Herb's second time on earth, and judging from his ideals and ambitions at present, he must have spent the interim in a place with an equatorial temperature. His saccharine smile early won for him an enviable position on the faculty, to which no reference need here be given. Keep it up Tuck, and when the roll is called at 1:30 we'll all be with you in spirit if not in body.
text:
An Angel! or if not, an earthly paragon.
The inevitable whale heaved another sigh, wiggled its tail to and fro once or twice and behold! There on the salty seaweed stood Herb Tucker, perfection personified. This is Herb's second time on earth, and judging from his ideals and ambitions at present, he must have spent the interim in a place with an equatorial temperature. His saccharine smile early won for him an enviable position on the faculty, to which no reference need here be given. Keep it up Tuck, and when the roll is called at 1:30 we'll all be with you in spirit if not in body.

name: Tucker, Raymond Hodgdon
motto: Shake off this drowsy sleep.
description: I was in the first cane rush. Didn't tear down any posters, but got a tear on my arm instead. I went to both Freshmen and Sophomore banquets. I and Guy Smart captured Tom Twomey at twelve o'clock up near Dr. Grant's house the night of the posters. I got off on my Freshman picture alright.
text:
Shake off this drowsy sleep.
I was in the first cane rush. Didn't tear down any posters, but got a tear on my arm instead. I went to both Freshmen and Sophomore banquets. I and Guy Smart captured Tom Twomey at twelve o'clock up near Dr. Grant's house the night of the posters. I got off on my Freshman picture alright.

name: Watson, Myles Standish
motto: Aye, every inch a king!
description: King is a native. He has become so acclimated that it is his special delight to go swimming in November. Myles is not an ordinary kind of a king, but holds the exalted and noble position of King of the Onion Patch. King is rather athletically inclined. If you do not believe it just start a little rough house with him. Some day we predict that he will be mayor of Durham.
text:
Aye, every inch a king!
King is a native. He has become so acclimated that it is his special delight to go swimming in November. Myles is not an ordinary kind of a king, but holds the exalted and noble position of King of the Onion Patch. King is rather athletically inclined. If you do not believe it just start a little rough house with him. Some day we predict that he will be mayor of Durham.

name: Whittemore, Hollie Leon
motto: But still his tongue ran on.
description: "My name is Whittemore" was the cry that made "Hollie" famous. In fact, for a short time, aided by the proper stimulus, he seemed particularly proud of calling his name to the four winds of heaven. Freshman year "Hollie" had many tribulations in his work. He could not decide which was most important, Dover, Newmarket, or Durham, but now he is settled under the motherly wing of "Ray" and will stand without hitching.
text:
But still his tongue ran on.
"My name is Whittemore" was the cry that made "Hollie" famous. In fact, for a short time, aided by the proper stimulus, he seemed particularly proud of calling his name to the four winds of heaven. Freshman year "Hollie" had many tribulations in his work. He could not decide which was most important, Dover, Newmarket, or Durham, but now he is settled under the motherly wing of "Ray" and will stand without hitching.

name: Wood, Arthur Gale
motto: While his off-heel, insidiously aside Provokes the caper which he seems to chide.
description: "Reddy" needs no light to show him his way through the streets of Durham; he carries his own. With his easy sylph-like gait he soon made a hit with the ladies. But this hit soon became a blow when Arthur began to dance. Oh, my! He became actually lovable when he said, "Do you know, I think I just dance naturally?" He does, and he can't help it. "Reddy" is strong on "Billy's" courses and expects to head the psychology department when he draws his "B.S."
text:
While his off-heel, insidiously aside Provokes the caper which he seems to chide.
"Reddy" needs no light to show him his way through the streets of Durham; he carries his own. With his easy sylph-like gait he soon made a hit with the ladies. But this hit soon became a blow when Arthur began to dance. Oh, my! He became actually lovable when he said, "Do you know, I think I just dance naturally?" He does, and he can't help it. "Reddy" is strong on "Billy's" courses and expects to head the psychology department when he draws his "B.S."
